stories.headline: US prosecutors drop criminal probe into Fed chair Powell
stories.summary: Breakthrough removes potential hurdle to Kevin Warsh’s confirmation as next head of the central bank

stories.headline: Fmr. Fed General Counsel Discusses DOJ's Powell Probe
stories.summary: Scott Alvarez, former general counsel at the Federal Reserve Board and adjunct professor at the George Washington School of Law, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The Justice Department is ending a controversial investigation into building-renovation cost overruns at the Federal Reserve, potentially clearing a path to confirmation for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s pick to be the next chair of the central bank. (Source: Bloomberg

stories.headline: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Iran War Impact on Credit Markets | Real Yield 4/24/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: BMO Capital Markets Head of US Rates Strategy Ian Lyngen, Moody's Rating Global Head of Private Credit Marc Pinto, Former Federal Reserve General Counsel Scott Alvarez, Barings High Yield Portfolio Manager Michael Best, and Neuberger Head of Private Debt Susan Kasser.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed chair Jerome Powell
stories.summary: The announcement on Friday is expected to clear the path for the confirmation of his successor, Kevin Warsh.

stories.headline: Can Europe help the Middle East resolve the war on Iran?
stories.summary: Al Jazeera’s Rory Challands reports from the EU summit in Cyprus.

stories.headline: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
stories.summary: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

stories.headline: US Sanctions China Refinery, Iran Shadow Fleet Ahead of Talks
stories.summary: The US sanctioned a Chinese oil refinery as well as dozens of shipping firms and vessels that transport Iranian oil, as the Trump administration ramps up economic pressure on Tehran ahead of diplomatic talks.

stories.headline: Treasuries Gain as DOJ Drops Fed Probe, Opening Path for Warsh
stories.summary: Treasuries gained for the first time in a week after the Justice Department dropped its investigation into the Federal Reserve, potentially opening a path for President Donald Trump’s pick Kevin Warsh to become Fed chair and push for interest-rate cuts.

stories.headline: Islamabad Return Signals Iran, US 'Don't Want to Go Back to War,' Says Amb. Herzog
stories.summary:
Amb. Michael Herzog, former Israeli Ambassador to the US, said the decisions from the US and Iran to send envoys back to Pakistan signal that they countries 'don't want to go back to war. '

resident Donald Trump will send envoys to Pakistan with the intention of meeting with Iranian officials, while Tehran sounded a pessimistic tone on the prospects for talks to end the eight-week war roiling the global economy.

stories.headline: Witkoff, Kushner Heading to Pakistan for More Talks
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is sending special envoy Steve Witkoff and his son-in-law Jared Kushner to Pakistan to hold another round of talks with Iran. This as Israel and Lebanon extend their own ceasefire. Bloomberg's Dan Williams has more from Jerusalem. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran War Has Drained U.S. Supplies of Critical, Costly Weapons
stories.summary: The Pentagon’s rush to rearm its Mideast forces makes it less ready to confront potential adversaries like Russia and China, administration and congressional officials say.

stories.headline: How Israel targeted and killed Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil
stories.summary: Amal Khalil was killed while reporting on an Israeli air strike in southern Lebanon during a 10-day ceasefire.

stories.headline: Iran-Iraq Tanker War redux? Why the Strait of Hormuz crisis is different
stories.summary: The Tanker War between Iran and Iraq in the 1980s also disrupted global shipping. But the situation today is different.

stories.headline: US envoys Witkoff, Kushner to head to Pakistan for Iran talks on Saturday, White House says
stories.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to arrive in Islamabad on Friday night, an official source in Pakistan said, without providing details about who he was likely to meet.

stories.headline: EU leaders vow to boost security and economic ties with Middle East to minimise effect of Iran war
stories.summary: Leaders from Lebanon, Egypt, Syria and Jordan as well as the secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council, were in Nicosia to meet their European Union counterparts on the sidelines of an EU summit.

stories.headline: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.headline: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.

stories.headline: Philippines' Duterte to stand trial as ICC confirms crimes against humanity charges
stories.summary: Ex-president Rodrigo Duterte is accused of the extrajudicial killing of thousands during his "war on drugs".

stories.headline: Prince Harry visits Kyiv to back Ukraine and spotlight mine clearance
stories.summary: Ukraine welcomed Prince Harry to Kyiv on Thursday 23 April 2026 for a two day visit that remained unannounced until his arrival.

stories.headline: EU leaders look to little-known mutual assistance pact amid Trump Nato jibes

stories.headline: PM Sanchez rebuffs US idea of suspending Spain from NATO
stories.summary: PM Sanchez responded to reports that the US may suspend Madrid from NATO over its opposition to the war on Iran.

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.headline: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses

stories.headline: Brazil Setting Higher Ethanol Blend to Stem Fuel Impact From War
stories.summary: Brazil is taking action to increase the use of ethanol in gasoline in an effort to tame the impacts of rising fuel costs as the war in the Middle East drags on.

stories.headline: Trump Says He Dislikes Prediction Markets. His Family Invests in Them.
stories.summary: The White House has warned staff not to wager on government decisions, but his family’s involvement with these firms undermines the president’s message.

stories.summary: President Trump’s nominee to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve wants to overhaul the central bank, including its more than $6 trillion balance sheet.

stories.headline: UK assisted dying bill fails after delays but advocates vow to try again
stories.summary: Supporters of bill to legalise assisted dying for terminally ill people say they will try again to pass legislation.

stories.headline: UK House of Lords accused of 'obstructionism' as time runs out on assisted dying bill
stories.summary: MPs in the House of Commons had backed legalising euthanasia for adults who have been given less than six months to live and can clearly express a wish to die, in a historic vote last June.

stories.headline: Brazil Blocks Polymarket in Crackdown on Prediction Sites
stories.summary: Brazil’s government blocked access to Polymarket and two dozen other prediction market platforms that it said were out of compliance with federal gambling laws.

stories.headline: Nasdaq 100 Heads for Record After Intel’s Blockbuster Guidance
stories.summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index was on track for a fresh all-time closing high on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks. Meanwhile, traders optimism about the Middle East increased as Iran’s foreign minister said he was headed to Pakistan.

stories.headline: Maine Governor Vetoes Bill That Would Have Paused New Data Centers
stories.summary: Gov. Janet Mills said she rejected what would have been the nation’s first moratorium on data centers because it failed to exempt a project in a distressed mill town.

stories.headline: Tariffs Raised Consumers’ Prices, but the Refunds Go Only to Businesses
stories.summary: Many families felt the sting of the president’s now-illegal tariffs, but companies have said little about whether they will share the $166 billion coming back to them.

stories.headline: A New Worry for Republicans: Latino Catholics Offended by Trump
stories.summary: In 2024, the president made significant inroads with Hispanics. But in a competitive Arizona congressional district, voters now express dismay at his feud with the pope.

stories.summary: Mr. Blanche’s actions are meant to demonstrate progress on the president’s priorities, chief among them payback.

stories.headline: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
stories.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

stories.headline: Gold Gains on Iran Talks Optimism With Fed Outlook in Focus
stories.summary: Gold advanced on optimism that the US and Iran are moving toward talks after days of deadlock, with traders also asessing the Federal Reserve’s interest rate path after the Justice Department dropped a probe of Chair Jerome Powell.

stories.summary: The polls are taking place against a controversy over a sweeping revision of electoral rolls.

stories.summary: Hopes that the US and Iran will be back to the negotiating table drove stocks to all-time highs, with the market also rising after a blowout forecast from Intel Corp. and the closing of a probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

stories.headline: Trump’s Dreams for a Battleship Led to His Navy Secretary’s Ouster
stories.summary: The Navy secretary, John Phelan, was supposed to deliver the first of the president’s ships by 2028. The timeline was nearly impossible.

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
stories.summary: Vlad Plahotniuc, once Moldova's richest man, was found guilty of partaking in a fraud which involved around 12% of the country's GDP.

stories.headline: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
stories.summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

stories.headline: A Kashmir tourist hotspot became a bloodbath. A year on, the pain remains unbearable
stories.summary: A year after militants killed 26 people in Indian-administered Kashmir, families are trying to cope with their loss.

stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.
